_intro.text:
One adapter instance manages exactly one gateway installation.
Use the connection values printed by the gateway installer.

mqttHost.label: Gateway host
mqttHost.help:
LAN hostname or IP address of the Raspberry Pi that runs the unofficial community gateway for SANlight Mesh and its local MQTT broker.
Use localhost only when ioBroker and the complete gateway run on the same host.

gatewayId.label: Gateway ID
gatewayId.help:
Enter the exact gateway ID printed by the gateway installer.
This instance ignores every other gateway ID.

mqttUsername.label: MQTT username
mqttUsername.help: Enter the ioBroker MQTT username printed by the gateway installer.

mqttPassword.label: MQTT password
mqttPassword.help: Enter the protected ioBroker MQTT password created by the gateway installer.

mqttTls.label: Use MQTT TLS
mqttTls.help: Leave disabled for the documented authenticated trusted-LAN setup on port 1883.

mqttRejectUnauthorized.label: Verify MQTT server certificate
mqttRejectUnauthorized.help:
Keep enabled.
Disable only for a deliberately trusted test setup with a self-signed certificate.

mqttCaPath.label: Custom CA certificate path
mqttCaPath.help:
Optional path on the ioBroker host.
Leave empty to use the operating system trust store.

topicPrefix.label: Topic prefix
topicPrefix.help:
Keep sanlightmesh/v1 unless the gateway was deliberately configured with another prefix.
Wildcards are not accepted.

brightnessDebounceMs.label: Brightness debounce in milliseconds
brightnessDebounceMs.help:
Combines rapid slider changes before sending one command.
The gateway still enforces its own safety interval.

allowBlackout.label: Enable emergency blackout controls
allowBlackout.help:
Leave disabled for normal operation.
Enabling this exposes commands that can immediately switch all controlled lamps off. The gateway still requires its protected blackout confirmation workflow.
